mechanism_narrative: Sustained monthly payroll growth above 150,000 persons signals robust labor demand and consumer income expansion, which drives equity valuations higher through increased corporate earnings expectations and consumer spending. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 20-day forward returns when PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth.
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS at 135046.0 thousand as of Feb 2013 indicates ongoing post-recession employment recovery
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization
failure_condition: Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or positive PAYEMS growth fails to produce positive 20d returns in > 55% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate remains below 0.25% for consecutive months, the zero lower bound constraint signals sustained monetary accommodation. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive equity returns as ultra-low rates compress discount rates and encourage risk-asset allocation, but the effect weakens if inflation expectations rise above 2.5% annually.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.15% as of Feb 2013 confirms ZLB regime
regime_hint: zero lower bound monetary accommodation with subdued inflation
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds 2.5%, the positive return premium from ZLB conditions disappears (Rank IC < 0.02 in high-inflation subperiods). Or SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of ZLB-triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month decline in housing starts (HOUST) below 800 thousand units signals weakening construction demand and forward-looking economic pessimism. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward in response to housing weakness.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls below 800 thousand units or declines > 5% MoM
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST at 890.0 thousand as of Jan 2013 shows recovery trajectory
regime_hint: post-crisis housing recovery with cyclical volatility
failure_condition: HOUST declines below 800k in > 3 consecutive months without producing negative 20d returns in > 60% of instances. Or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth decelerates below 0.2% MoM while simultaneously the unemployment rate rises above 7.5%, signaling a supply-side contraction coupled with labor market softening. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts negative 20-day forward returns as the combination indicates stagflationary pressure and reduced corporate profit margins.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO MoM growth < 0.2% AND UNRATE > 7.5% simultaneously
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 99.4965 (2017=100) and UNRATE at 7.7% as of Feb 2013
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market slack and supply constraints
failure_condition: The joint condition (INDPRO < 0.2% MoM AND UNRATE > 7.5%) occurs in > 5 instances without producing negative 20d returns in > 55% of cases. Or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: Real retail sales (RSAFS) growth accelerates above 0.5% MoM, indicating robust consumer spending and demand-side momentum. This always-on trend mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns as accelerating consumption signals earnings growth and validates equity valuations in a demand-driven expansion.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 421399.0 million as of Feb 2013 reflects post-recession consumer recovery
regime_hint: demand-led expansion with consumer confidence recovery
failure_condition: RSAFS MoM growth > 0.5% fails to produce positive 60d returns in > 58% of instances. Or Rank IC < 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the consumer price index (CPIAUCSL) above 2.5% YoY triggers expectations of future Federal Reserve tightening, which compresses equity valuations through higher discount rates. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative 5-day forward returns as market participants front-run anticipated policy normalization.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 232.77 (1982-1984=100) as of Feb 2013 implies ~2.0% YoY inflation
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL YoY growth > 2.5% occurs in > 4 instances without producing negative 5d returns in > 60% of cases. Or Rank IC < 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: The unemployment rate (UNRATE) declines by more than 0.3 percentage points over a 3-month rolling window, signaling accelerating labor market tightening and wage pressure. This regime-conditional mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns in early-cycle expansions but negative returns in late-cycle regimes where wage inflation threatens profit margins.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 7.7% as of Feb 2013 remains elevated relative to pre-crisis levels
regime_hint: post-shock recovery with labor market normalization and low wage inflation
failure_condition: UNRATE declines > 0.3pp in 3-month window produces positive 60d returns in < 55% of instances during recovery regimes. Or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 18-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) accelerates above 2.5% annualized while simultaneously housing starts (HOUST) exceed 900 thousand units, signaling broad-based demand expansion with construction momentum. This interaction-driven mechanism predicts positive 60-day forward returns as the dual confirmation of macro strength validates equity risk premiums and earnings growth.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 QoQ annualized growth > 2.5% AND HOUST > 900 thousand units
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 at 13656.8 billion (2017 dollars) as of Q4 2012, HOUST at 890.0 thousand as of Jan 2013
regime_hint: mid-cycle expansion with synchronized demand and construction recovery
failure_condition: The joint condition (GDPC1 > 2.5% annualized AND HOUST > 900k) occurs in > 4 instances without producing positive 60d returns in > 60% of cases. Or Rank IC of interaction term < 0.03.
